questions.question: Suppose we have a random sample of size n = 25 from a population with unknown mean μ and variance σ². Using the central limit theorem, find the probability that the sample mean is within 1 standard error of the true mean. Assume that the population standard deviation is unknown. Round the answer to 2 decimal places. (3 marks)

A company claims that the average height of its employees is 175 cm with a standard deviation of 5 cm. From a random sample of 36 employees, the sample mean height is 178.2 cm with a sample standard deviation of 4.8 cm. Using a confidence interval for the mean, find the probability that the true mean height of all employees lies within the interval (168.6, 187.8) cm, assuming a 95% confidence level. (5 marks)

Given a random sample of size n = 30 from a population with known variance σ² = 9. Using the central limit theorem, find the z-score corresponding to a confidence interval (x̄ - 2, x̄ + 2) for the population mean, assuming a 99% confidence level. (4 marks)
